skill.name: happyhorse-1-0
skill.description: Generate text-to-video with HappyHorse 1.0 through the local RunComfy CLI. The skill documents supported inputs, prompt guidance, output handling, and model selection guidance for short videos with synchronized audio.
skill.summary: Create short HappyHorse 1.0 videos on RunComfy from text prompts.
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: Static findings for Ruby backtick execution and weak cryptography are false positives caused by Markdown code spans, examples, arrows, and prose in SKILL.md. The confirmed risks are intentional RunComfy CLI execution, outbound HTTPS API calls, bearer token handling, and downloaded output files, so users should publish with a clear external-service warning.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Invocation Sends User Prompts to RunComfy
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run the local RunComfy CLI for HappyHorse text-to-video generation. This is expected behavior, but it executes an external command and submits user prompt content to a third-party API.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The invocation examples and workflow description explicitly show runcomfy commands and POST submission to the RunComfy model API. The behavior is intentional, not hidden, but it has privacy and execution risk.

medium_findings.title: Bearer Token and Local Token File Handling
medium_findings.description: The skill documents RunComfy login, RUNCOMFY_TOKEN use, and token storage under a hidden user configuration path. This is legitimate for authenticated API access, but marketplace users should understand that credentials are required and stored or provided through the environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly references a bearer token, RUNCOMFY_TOKEN, and ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json. This confirms credential handling, while the documented 0600 mode reduces severity.

medium_findings.title: Generated Output Downloads to Local Filesystem
medium_findings.description: The skill directs the CLI to download generated outputs into a caller-supplied output directory. The documented host whitelist and file size cap are helpful controls, but the workflow still writes remote model output to local storage.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples require --output-dir and the workflow describes downloading whitelisted URLs. The risk is bounded by documented controls but remains relevant for disk usage and file provenance.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded RunComfy URLs Are Service Documentation
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to RunComfy home, model, documentation, and API endpoints that match the skill purpose. No evidence found of covert callbacks, unrelated domains, or suspicious exfiltration endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited domains are RunComfy documentation and model API domains aligned with the declared function. The skill also states a narrow outbound endpoint policy.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick and Weak Crypto Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged many Markdown backticks as Ruby command execution and weak cryptography in prose. Review found documentation, shell examples, arrows, and model descriptions, with no executable Ruby code or cryptographic implementation in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only scanned file is Markdown, and the cited lines are front matter, examples, and prose. No evidence found of Ruby code execution or weak hash usage.

dangerous_patterns.title: Third-Party CLI Execution Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill relies on npx installation guidance and runcomfy CLI commands. Agents should only execute these commands when the user requests video generation and accepts RunComfy account use.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and central to the skill. They are not malicious by themselves, but they create an external command execution dependency.

dangerous_patterns.title: Outbound Model API Submission Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow sends prompts and bearer-token authenticated requests to RunComfy model API endpoints. This requires user awareness because prompt content leaves the local environment.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The skill describes the API endpoint, polling, result retrieval, and outbound endpoint set. This directly confirms network submission behavior.

content.value_statement: Teams need clear guidance for short AI video generation without guessing model parameters. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through HappyHorse 1.0 prompts, schema choices, and RunComfy CLI execution.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to choose HappyHorse 1.0 instead of related video models.
Documents the text-to-video endpoint, input fields, defaults, and allowed values.
Provides RunComfy CLI examples for landscape, vertical, and lower-cost test runs.
Gives prompt guidance for motion, camera language, multi-shot consistency, and audio direction.
Describes RunComfy API polling, output downloads, exit codes, and token handling.
limitations: Requires a working RunComfy account, CLI installation, and valid authentication.
Uses the text-to-video endpoint only, not image-to-video or external audio control.
Generated clips are limited to the documented duration, aspect ratio, and resolution options.
Prompts and job metadata are sent to RunComfy, not processed fully offline.

use_cases.title: Create Brand Story Clips
use_cases.description: Generate short multi-shot product or brand videos with consistent visual anchors and synchronized ambient audio.

use_cases.title: Prototype Social Video Concepts
use_cases.description: Test vertical or square short-form video ideas with clear duration, aspect ratio, and resolution settings.

use_cases.title: Evaluate AI Video Prompts
use_cases.description: Compare prompt variants by reusing seeds, changing one visual beat, and reviewing RunComfy outputs.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Video Clip
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one short video.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use HappyHorse 1.0 to generate a 5 second 16:9 1080P video of [scene]. Include simple motion and ambient audio.

prompt_templates.title: Vertical Social Short
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a mobile-first clip with specific format settings.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 9:16 HappyHorse video for [platform]. Use 8 seconds, no watermark, and describe one clear action over time.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Shot Character Scene
prompt_templates.scenario: Maintain character consistency across two beats.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a two-shot HappyHorse prompt. Keep [character details] identical in both shots, then change the action and location.

prompt_templates.title: Production Variant Pass
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced iteration with cost and comparison controls.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a 720P 3 second HappyHorse test for [concept], reuse seed [seed], then prepare a 1080P version after review.

output_examples.input: A user asks for a cinematic five second coffee shop video.
output_examples.output: The assistant chooses HappyHorse 1.0, writes a motion-focused prompt, selects 16:9 1080P, and runs the RunComfy CLI with an output directory.

output_examples.input: A creator needs a vertical ad with one consistent character.
output_examples.output: The assistant formats a 9:16 prompt with repeated character anchors, eight seconds duration, ambient audio direction, and watermark preference.

output_examples.input: A team wants a low-cost preview before final rendering.
output_examples.output: The assistant recommends a 720P three second test pass, keeps the seed for comparison, and prepares the final 1080P settings.

best_practices: Describe motion over time instead of only describing a still image.
Use one primary visual beat per clip when testing prompt quality.
Confirm RunComfy authentication and output directory before running generation.
anti_patterns: Do not use unsupported aspect ratios, resolutions, or durations.
Do not include private or sensitive content in prompts without approval.
Do not stack many unrelated actions into one short clip.

faq.question: Does this skill run video generation locally?
faq.answer: No. It uses the local RunComfy CLI to submit jobs to the RunComfy Model API.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it support image-to-video?
faq.answer: This skill documents the text-to-video endpoint only. Image-to-video requires a separate pipeline.

faq.question: What video lengths are documented?
faq.answer: The documented duration range is 3 to 15 seconds, with a default of 5 seconds.

faq.question: What security warning should users know?
faq.answer: Prompts and authenticated requests are sent to RunComfy, and generated files are downloaded locally.

faq.question: Can it generate synchronized audio?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill describes in-pass synchronized audio through HappyHorse 1.0, including ambient sound and dialogue direction.
